sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Jacqueline, presented with anxiety about recent blood work showing hepatitis C.
She reports feeling fine but surprised by the diagnosis.
She has a history of IV drug use, but has been clean for over 15 years.
She currently drinks one beer a day and smokes one to two cigarettes a day.
She has a history of hypertension, diabetes, and depression, with a family history of the same conditions.
She reports feeling tired at the end of the day despite getting enough sleep.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of IV drug use, which she has been clean from for over 15 years.
She has hypertension, diabetes, and depression.
She also has a family history of hypertension, diabetes, and depression.
Review of systems: The patient reports feeling tired at the end of the day, but does not have any other symptoms.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient appears in no distress and no jaundice on skin exam.
Heart exam revealed a regular rate and rhythm with a 2/6 systolic murmur, which has not changed from last year.
Lung exam was clear with no wheezes, rales, or rhonchi.
Abdominal exam revealed present bowel sounds, a soft abdomen, and no hepatosplenomegaly.
Musculoskeletal exam was unremarkable.
Test Results: The patient's HCV antibody test was positive, and her liver panel showed an elevated AST at 39, but normal albumin and total bilirubin levels.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a confirmed diagnosis of hepatitis C, a viral infection that affects the liver, which she likely contracted years ago.
Problem list: Hepatitis C; Hypertension; Diabetes; Depression.
Plan:
I prescribed a liver ultrasound to determine the severity of liver disease and to check for fibrosis.
I also ordered a blood test to check for HCV RNA and HCV genotype to confirm the diagnosis and determine the best course of treatment.
I instructed the patient to have her family members, including her wife and children, undergo the same screening as she has had.
Follow-up: I will see the patient in three weeks to discuss the results of the additional testing and to determine the best course of treatment for her hepatitis C.
